title: Immersive Web SDK AI-Native Development Documentation
citation: Immersive Web SDK AI-Native Development documentation, accessed 2026-05-04.
permitted_uses: Planning optional local XR agent tooling for screenshots, controller input simulation, scene inspection, ECS debugging, and Quest-oriented development workflow experiments.
claims_supported: As accessed on 2026-05-04, the documentation showed IWSDK documentation version v0.3.1.
The documentation says IWSDK exposes 32 MCP tools for AI-assisted WebXR development, including screenshots, controller input, scene inspection, and ECS debugging.
The documentation describes a managed Playwright browser, an iwsdk mcp stdio server, and Vite development integration coordinated by iwsdk dev up.
The documentation says iwsdk adapter sync writes workspace-based MCP config files so AI tools can discover the runtime MCP server.
The documentation describes three development modes: Agent, Oversight, and Collaborate.
The documentation states the normal browser can run independently while the agent works in the background.
The documentation says Codex adapter generation targets .codex/config.toml when ai.tools includes codex.
The documentation recommends checking xr_get_session_status first before other MCP runtime actions.

The documentation says optional @iwsdk/reference warmup downloads a pinned model and reference corpus, and optional @meta-quest/hzdb can add Meta Quest device management, asset search, and IWSDK documentation lookup.
claims_not_supported: That the MCP tools are available to Codex in this workspace without installing IWSDK packages and adapter configuration.
That @iwsdk/reference warmup is free of network/model-download or storage implications.
That @meta-quest/hzdb has an acceptable distribution license for OpenClinXR.
That IWSDK MCP emulation replaces physical Quest 3 smoke tests for frame pacing, comfort, or controller behavior.
